page: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor and a dedicated NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU. The CPU architecture provides 12 virtual CPUs across 6 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 2.2 GHz. It features a cache hierarchy consisting of 32 KB L1d, 1 MB L2, and 38 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 85.0 GB of system memory, yielding 7.08 GB per core, and the GPU is provisioned with 40 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is not bundled with this instance type.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct contrast between CPU-bound and GPU-accelerated workloads. Single-core CPU performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by low scores in single-threaded stress-ng, gzip compression, and Geekbench single-core tests. However, multi-core CPU execution and memory bandwidth fall into the average tier, with memory read bandwidth reaching 528.86 GB/sec for cached small blocks. The server excels in GPU-driven tasks, achieving top-tier performance in LLM inference benchmarks. For a medium 7B model, it delivers prompt processing speeds of 3469.43 tokens per second and text generation speeds of 150.32 tokens per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of GPU resources and system memory relative to the modest 12-vCPU count makes this instance highly specialized. It avoids the overhead of larger CPU configurations while still providing access to a high-end enterprise GPU. This resource distribution makes the server suitable for machine learning inference, small-to-medium LLM deployment, and GPU-accelerated data science tasks, whereas it represents a poor tradeoff for general-purpose single-threaded CPU computing or storage-heavy database applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for GPU-intensive workloads. It features an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 12 vCPUs, 6 physical cores, and 85.0 GB of system memory. The server is equipped with a dedicated NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU containing 40 GB of VRAM, while local storage is absent. Benchmark data highlights a clear performance tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is weak, but the server achieves top-tier results in LLM inference, processing 3469.43 tokens per second for medium 7B models. Multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for organizations requiring high-end GPU acceleration without paying for excessive CPU cores. It is best suited for machine learning inference, data science, and medium-scale language model execution.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 12 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 85GB RAM, and an NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU with 40GB VRAM, built for high-performance machine learning inference.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized server with 12 vCPUs, 85GB RAM, and a 40GB NVIDIA A100 GPU for machine learning.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized server pairing a dedicated forty-gigabyte graphics processor with eighty-five gigabytes of memory for high-throughput machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Equipped with one NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPU featuring 40 GB of VRAM
Features 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 85 GB of system memory
Delivers top-tier LLM inference speeds for small and medium models
Exhibits weak single-core CPU performance but average multi-core capabilities
Contains no local storage, requiring external storage solutions
Optimized for machine learning inference and GPU-accelerated data science
